name: Legal Contract Clause Risk Assessment Benchmark
description: Tests an agent's ability to identify risk levels and specific legal issues in contract clauses under US jurisdiction. Covers non-competes, indemnification, liability, termination, IP assignment, confidentiality, force majeure, and dispute resolution clauses. Agents must classify risk and enumerate concrete issues.
sourceDescription: Contract clauses are synthetic but modeled on common commercial patterns. Risk classifications reflect general US contract law principles, not specific case law or legal advice.

tests.description: Non-compete with unreasonable geographic scope and duration — high risk

body.clause: Employee agrees that for a period of five (5) years following termination of employment for any reason, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in, own, manage, operate, control, be employed by, participate in, or be connected with any business that competes with the Company anywhere in the world. This restriction applies regardless of whether termination is voluntary or involuntary, with or without cause.

Each party (the "Indemnifying Party") agrees to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the other party and its officers, directors, and employees (the "Indemnified Party") from and against any third-party claims, damages, losses, and reasonable expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or related to the Indemnifying Party's (a) material breach of this Agreement, (b) gross negligence or willful misconduct, or (c) violation of applicable law. The Indemnified Party shall provide prompt written notice and reasonable cooperation.

Service Provider shall be liable for any and all damages, whether direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, special, punitive, or exemplary, arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the services provided hereunder, without any limitation whatsoever. This includes but is not limited to lost profits, lost revenue, loss of data, business interruption, and reputational harm, regardless of whether Service Provider was advised of the possibility of such damages.

tests.description: Reasonable limitation of liability with mutual cap — low risk

body.clause: EXCEPT FOR EACH PARTY'S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS AND BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY, NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. EACH PARTY'S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNTS PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO SERVICE PROVIDER DURING THE TWELVE (12) MONTH PERIOD IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM.

tests.description: Auto-renewal without adequate notice provision — medium risk

body.clause: This Agreement shall have an initial term of one (1) year and shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. Pricing for renewal terms shall be determined by Provider at its sole discretion and communicated within thirty (30) days of renewal. Provider may increase fees by any amount upon renewal.

tests.description: Unilateral termination for convenience with short cure period — medium risk

body.clause: Provider may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason, upon five (5) business days' written notice to Client. Client may terminate this Agreement only for cause, and only after providing Provider with sixty (60) days' written notice and an opportunity to cure. Upon termination by Provider, Client shall pay all fees through the end of the then-current billing cycle plus an early termination fee equal to three (3) months of service fees.

tests.description: IP assignment without carve-outs for prior work or personal projects — high risk

body.clause: Employee hereby irrevocably assigns to the Company all right, title, and interest in and to any and all inventions, works of authorship, ideas, discoveries, improvements, trade secrets, and other intellectual property, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, that Employee conceives, develops, or reduces to practice at any time during the term of employment. This assignment includes work created outside of business hours and using Employee's own equipment, and extends to any intellectual property conceived within twelve (12) months following termination.

tests.description: Standard confidentiality with reasonable scope and duration — low risk

body.clause: Each party agrees to hold in confidence all Confidential Information received from the other party during the term of this Agreement. "Confidential Information" means non-public information designated as confidential or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential. Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving party; (b) was known to the receiving party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of Confidential Information; or (d) is received from a third party without restriction. This obligation shall survive for three (3) years following termination.

tests.description: Standard force majeure clause — low risk

body.clause: Neither party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations under this Agreement to the extent such failure or delay results from circumstances beyond the reasonable control of that party, including but not limited to acts of God, natural disasters, war, terrorism, riots, embargoes, acts of civil or military authorities, fire, floods, epidemics, strikes, or failures of telecommunications or power supply. The affected party shall provide prompt notice and use reasonable efforts to mitigate the effects. If the force majeure event continues for more than ninety (90) days, either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice.

tests.description: Arbitration with unfavorable venue and cost allocation — medium risk

body.clause: Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration administered by a private arbitration firm selected by Provider, conducted in Provider's home jurisdiction of Singapore. The arbitration shall be conducted in English by a single arbitrator appointed by Provider. Client shall bear all costs of arbitration including the arbitrator's fees, Provider's reasonable attorneys' fees, and travel expenses. The arbitrator's decision shall be final and non-appealable. Client waives any right to participate in a class action.

tests.description: Data processing clause with inadequate breach notification — high risk

body.clause: Provider may process Client's personal data in any jurisdiction Provider deems appropriate and may engage sub-processors without notice to or consent from Client. Provider shall notify Client of a data breach within a reasonable time after discovery, but in no event shall Provider be liable for any damages arising from a breach. Client acknowledges that Provider makes no warranties regarding the security of personal data. Client shall indemnify Provider against any claims by data subjects or regulatory authorities arising from Provider's processing of Client's data.

tests.description: Non-solicitation with reasonable scope — low risk

body.clause: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months following its termination, neither party shall directly solicit for employment any employee of the other party with whom it had material contact during the course of the engagement. This restriction does not apply to (a) general recruitment advertising not targeted at specific individuals, (b) employees who independently respond to job postings, or (c) employees who were terminated by the other party prior to solicitation.

Provider reserves the right to modify any terms of this Agreement at any time by posting updated terms on its website or by sending notice to the email address on file. Continued use of the services after such modification constitutes Client's acceptance of the modified terms. Provider is not required to highlight or summarize changes. If Client does not agree with any modification, Client's sole remedy is to terminate the Agreement, subject to any applicable early termination fees.

tests.description: Warranty disclaimer with limited remedies — medium risk

body.clause: THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT. Provider does not warrant that the services will be uninterrupted, error-free, or secure. Client's exclusive remedy for any defect or failure of the services shall be re-performance of the defective services, and if re-performance fails, a pro-rata refund for the affected period. In no event shall Provider's total liability exceed fees paid in the prior month.

tests.description: Balanced SLA with clear remedies — low risk

body.clause: Provider guarantees 99.9% monthly uptime for the services, measured as total available minutes minus downtime minutes divided by total minutes in the calendar month. Scheduled maintenance windows (communicated at least 72 hours in advance) are excluded from downtime calculations. If uptime falls below 99.9% in any calendar month, Client shall receive a service credit equal to 10% of that month's fees for each 0.1% below the target, up to a maximum credit of 30% of monthly fees. Credits must be requested within 30 days and will be applied to subsequent invoices.
